pattern: A membership test you can ask instantly turns a step-by-step doubling chase into a short loop: keep doubling while the current value still shows up in the collection, then report the first value that doesn't.
hints: You'll be checking 'is this value present in the array' over and over -- pick a data structure that answers that in constant time rather than rescanning the array each round.
Starting from the given value, loop: if it's present, double it and continue; if not, that's your answer.
Because values only grow, the loop ends quickly -- there's no need to bound the number of iterations manually, the membership check does that for you.

edge_inputs.description: original not present at all, returned unchanged
chain of doublings found repeatedly before stopping
smallest possible original and nums values
original present but its double is not, stops after a single doubling
array contains original's double but not original itself
original at the upper bound with no chain to follow
duplicates of original in the array don't extend the chain further
long chain that keeps doubling until it exceeds every value in the array

stress.description: dense array covering the full value range so doubling chains run long before stopping
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    original = 1
    return [nums, original]
